item.title: World’s Most Fervent Day Traders in Korea to Get Risky New Tools
item.description: The world’s best-performing yet most volatile market is set to debut its first ever single-stock leveraged exchange‑traded funds this week, tools that have the potential to amplify gains and losses.

item.title: Congo Faces Surge in Ebola Cases as Key Medical Tools Run Short
item.description: Africa’s top public health official said the latest Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo is being fought without one of the most important tools in epidemic response: vaccines.

item.title: More War-Driven Inflation Seen in Fed’s Favored Gauge
item.description: The Federal Reserve’s favored top-line inflation gauge is rapidly approaching 4% as a war-driven spike in energy costs generates unease that price pressures will broaden.

item.title: Hungary’s New Premier Warns Predecessor Left Budget ‘Skeletons’
item.description: Hungary’s new Prime Minister Peter Magyar said he was hoping for economic growth of about 2% this year but put voters on notice that the state of public finances left by his predecessor made it impossible to make firm forecasts.

item.title: Hyperscaler Debt Flood Brings Derivatives Bonanza
item.description: As big tech companies raise hundreds of billions of dollars to fund artificial intelligence investments, Wall Street banks are increasingly finding they have to trade more credit derivatives to keep doing business with the hyperscalers.

item.title: France Bars Israel Minister Ben Gvir Over Treatment of Activists
item.description: France banned Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir from entering the country, citing threats and violence against activists who were trying to get aid to Gaza by sea.

item.title: Warsh’s Fed ‘Regime Change’ May Require Patience, Consensus
item.description: Kevin Warsh has vowed to usher in “regime change” as the new chair of the Federal Reserve, but Fed watchers say there are limits to how much he can revamp on his own — and that even in areas where he can make sweeping reform, it might be smarter for him to hold back.

item.title: US Says Its Blockade Against Iran Has Redirected 100 Vessels
item.description: US forces have redirected 100 commercial vessels during its six-week-long blockade of Iran’s ports, Central Command said.

item.title: Delivery Hero Says Uber Proposed €33 a Share Takeover Bid
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. proposed buying Delivery Hero SE for €33 ($38) a share as it seeks to better compete with DoorDash Inc. outside the US.

item.title: Trump’s 3,711 Trades Point to Multiple Stock-Market Strategies
item.description: Index-tracking and automated execution appear to be on show in transactions linked to the president.

item.title: Bangladesh Central Bank Launches $5 Billion Fund to Spur Growth
item.description: Bangladesh’s central bank unveiled a 600 billion-taka ($5 billion) package to boost economic growth, revive shuttered factories and support small businesses.

item.title: US Companies Shamed by Trump Tiptoe Into a Tariff-Refund Race
item.description: Companies in the US are wary of saying too much about one particular nugget of good news for their bottom line and inflation.

item.title: Nvidia CEO Urges Super Micro to Tighten Up Amid Taiwan Crackdown
item.description: Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang urged Super Micro Computer Inc. to tighten up on compliance after Taiwan detained three people this week for allegedly making fraudulent declarations about AI servers made by its US partner.

item.title: The World Loses Petrodollar Stimulus as Iran War Drains Gulf Wealth
item.description: In normal times, Gulf states sell oil and gas, spending some of the proceeds at home while investing the surplus abroad.

item.title: India Trade Minister to Visit Canada for FTA Talks, Meet Carney
item.description: India and Canada will hold talks on a proposed free trade agreement from May 25 to 27, Indian Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal told reporters on Saturday.

item.title: DeepSeek To Make Permanent 75% Discount on Flagship AI Model
item.description: DeepSeek said it will make permanent a steep discount on its flagship V4‑Pro model, maintaining prices for developers at a quarter of their original level.

item.title: Danish Economy Ministry Raises GDP Forecast Due to Pharma Sales
item.description: Denmark’s gross domestic product will expand at a faster pace than previously expected due to strong sales from the country’s pharmaceutical industry, which is dominated by Novo Nordisk A/S, the economy ministry said.

item.title: Charting the Global Economy: Factory Activity Sags on Inflation
item.description: The global economy is showing signs of wearing down with inflation pressures persisting during the third month of a war-induced energy crunch.

item.title: ECB Hike May Be Inevitable to Keep Credibility, Stournaras Says
item.description: Preserving the European Central Bank’s credibility is a strong argument in favor of an interest-rate increase next month, according to Governing Council member Yannis Stournaras.

item.title: Architect Norman Foster on Why the West Struggles to Build Big
item.description: What it takes to make great engineering and design breakthroughs at scale.

item.title: Odd Lots: Norman Foster on the Struggle to Build Big (Podcast)
item.description: Not many people think of designing buildings as an exercise in economics, but the entire process is defined by constraints around resources (both physical and financial), and an iconic building can also have a huge impact on the wealth and development of the area around it. So how do you encourage private developers to consider the public good when designing new projects? And how are some countries able to encourage more landmark building projects than others? In this episode, we speak with Norm

item.title: Rubio to Meet India’s Modi With Energy, Trade Ties in Focus
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio will meet Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Saturday, with energy security high on the agenda as the South Asian nation grapples with soaring oil prices and supply disruptions stemming from the war in Iran.

item.title: Hong Kong’s First Astronaut Joins Mission to China Space Station
item.description: Hong Kong’s first astronaut, a former police officer with a doctorate in computer forensics, will join a Chinese mission to its space station on Sunday, in what the city’s Chief Executive John Lee described as a “historic moment.”

item.title: Xi Urges Chinese Authorities to Curb Accidents After Mine Blast
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping urged authorities nationwide to step up efforts to curb major industrial accidents following a deadly gas explosion at a coal mine in northern Shanxi province, underscoring persistent safety risks in mining and heavy industry.

item.title: Ebola Outruns Containment in Eastern Congo as Contact Tracing Falters
item.description: Ebola is spreading faster than responders can track it in eastern Congo, where health workers managed to follow up with barely one in five identified contacts in a single day.

item.title: India Raises Diesel, Gasoline Prices for Third Time in Eight Days
item.description: India’s state-run refiners raised retail prices again of diesel and gasoline on Saturday to help processors cut losses on discounted sales and to control a spike in demand.

item.title: Indonesia Plans to Beat Global Trading Giants at Their Own Game
item.description: For years, Indonesia’s raw materials have been ferried from remote mines and plantations to global markets by armies of traders who handle negotiations, loans and even cranes and river barges.

item.title: New Zealand to Invest in Drones, Fleet to Shield Maritime Routes
item.description: New Zealand intends to spend about NZ$1.6 billion ($936 million) on drones, ship maintenance and naval upgrades to bolster the island nation’s maritime security at a time of increasing concern about supply routes.

item.title: Abrego Garcia Wins Dismissal of US Human-Smuggling Case
item.description: A federal judge in Tennessee dismissed human-smuggling charges against Salvadoran migrant Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia after concluding the US government brought the case in retaliation for his legal fight against being deported.

item.title: Zoom’s Anthropic Investment Has Netted the Company $1 Billion
item.description: Zoom Communications Inc., the videoconferencing company, has netted about $1 billion on an investment it made in artificial intelligence startup Anthropic PBC in early 2023.
